rag_rules: Assume and guarantee statements must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes
Assign statements in implementation scope must reference valid output features or declared auxiliary variables
Type consistency is required in AGREE expressions; do not introduce mismatched types or unresolved references
Only use visible identifiers from the current component scope when generating AGREE contracts
generic_rules: AGREE contracts should be placed in appropriate annex scopes based on whether they are type-level or implementation-level
Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type safety and avoid unresolved references
Repair diagnostics by aligning AGREE clauses with visible AADL identifiers and valid scopes
Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers unless they are present in the model
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax with valid statements like assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign
Implementation-level bindings use assign statements only for valid output features or declared variables
Type declarations in AGREE use int, real, and bool; avoid package-qualified or undefined scalar types
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions are resolvable within the current component scope
anti_patterns: Placing assume/guarantee statements in implementation scopes instead of component types
Using unresolved or package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model
Introducing mismatched types in AGREE expressions such as assigning int to bool
Adding new identifiers or types that do not exist in the visible AADL model
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and insertion location decisions
AADL package reference resolution beyond model dependencies
